team_context.cap_situation: tight — ~$12M in space per OTC, limited restructure flexibility due to Howie Roseman's pre-baked option bonus structures. Jordan Davis extension just signed ($78M/3yr). Jalen Carter extension imminent. A.J. Brown's $43.5M dead cap pre-June 1 complicates any trade. 2028 is the real cap crunch year.
team_context.trade_posture: Buyers at EDGE (lost Jaelan Phillips to Carolina), safety (lost Reed Blankenship to Houston). Could be sellers on A.J. Brown if relationship is irreparable. Will listen on veterans with expiring deals but core young defense is untouchable. Roseman is aggressive and creative — expect movement.

players.name: Jalen Hurts
players.performance_evidence: Super Bowl LIX MVP (2024). 3,224 passing yards, 25 TDs, 6 INTs, 421 rushing yards, 8 rushing TDs in 2025. PFF overall grade of 78.9 (11th among QBs). Won Super Bowl, led team to 11-6 in 2025 but offense regressed and lost in Wild Card. Not elite as a passer but the tush push and dual-threat ability keep him in Pro Bowl tier. No-trade clause in contract.

trade_value.reasoning: Franchise QB with no-trade clause and $107M dead cap. Effectively untradeable by both contractual restriction and financial reality. Value is theoretical — he cannot be moved.

players.availability_reasoning: No-trade clause makes this moot. $107M dead cap if traded. Super Bowl MVP on a locked-in deal through 2028. Zero chance of movement. Roseman likely to restructure/extend in 2026 or 2027 to create cap space.
players.injury_notes: Concussion history (missed time in 2024). Generally durable — played 16 games in 2025.

players.name: Tanner McKee
players.performance_evidence: Started Week 18 rest game in 2025. Minimal meaningful game action. Roseman traded Sam Howell away, leaving McKee as the primary backup. Considered a capable backup with starter upside by some evaluators.

trade_value.reasoning: Backup QB on a cheap deal with one year of control. Some teams might offer a late-round pick given his physical tools, but he has almost no proven production.

players.availability_reasoning: Eagles need a reliable backup behind Hurts. Not being actively shopped but if a team offered a 5th-round pick, they'd listen. Low priority either way.

players.name: Saquon Barkley
players.performance_evidence: Rushed for 2,000+ yards in 2024 (record-setting season), won Super Bowl. 1,140 rushing yards in 2025 (10th in NFL) in a slight step back but still elite. Extended with a 2-year, $41.2M deal making him highest-paid RB in history. First-Team All-Pro 2024.

trade_value.reasoning: Elite production but turning 29, plays RB (low positional value), and $38.8M dead cap makes a trade financially devastating. The cap hit absorbs most of his theoretical value. Worth a mid-2nd in pure talent/contract terms but dead cap makes him immovable.

players.availability_reasoning: Just extended in March 2025. $38.8M dead cap. Face of the franchise alongside Hurts. Absolutely no chance of being traded — the financials alone make it impossible.
players.injury_notes: Remarkably durable. No significant injuries since torn ACL in 2020.

players.name: Tank Bigsby
players.performance_evidence: Acquired from Jacksonville in Week 2 of 2025 for 5th and 6th round picks. Served as RB2/kick returner. Career-best 766 yards and 7 TDs in 2024 with JAX. Limited role behind Barkley in Philly. Not a receiving threat (8 career catches).

trade_value.reasoning: Cheap final-year backup RB who can return kicks. The Eagles just paid a 5th and 6th for him months ago. Worth a 6th-round pick on the trade market given the expiring deal and limited role.

players.availability_reasoning: Expiring contract, backup behind Barkley. If a team needed a physical runner and offered a late-round pick, Eagles would listen. Not a priority to keep.

players.name: A.J. Brown
players.performance_evidence: 78 catches, 1,000+ yards, 7 TDs in 2025 despite reported discontent. Three-time Pro Bowler, three-time Second-Team All-Pro. Still clearly talented at 28 but relationship with Hurts/organization has frayed — three drops and sideline incident in Wild Card loss. Production remains high but body language and engagement are concerning.

trade_value.reasoning: Still productive WR1 with 4 years of control, but massive $43.5M dead cap pre-June 1 means Eagles eat a historic cap hit to trade him. Post-June 1 saves only $7M. Realistic return is a conditional 3rd or 4th per beat reporters — the contract structure craters his trade value despite the talent. Acquiring team gets a good deal ($23M/yr cap hit) but Eagles pay an enormous price to move him.

players.availability_reasoning: Spotrac explicitly flagged Brown as a potential mess. Beat reporters say the relationship may be beyond repair after the Wild Card sideline incident and refusal to speak to media. Eagles are listening to trade calls per multiple reports. The obstacle isn't willingness — it's the $43.5M dead cap. A post-June 1 trade is more likely but eliminates draft-day compensation. This is the most complex trade situation on the roster.
players.injury_notes: Has dealt with knee issues in past seasons but played 16 games in 2025.
players.character_notes: Heated sideline confrontation with Nick Sirianni during Wild Card loss. Refused to speak to media after playoff exit. Questions about rapport with Hurts have persisted for two seasons. Three-time Pro Bowler talent, but the drama is real and publicly documented.
players.trade_buzz: Spotrac, PhillyVoice, and multiple beat reporters have written extensively about trade scenarios. Ravens mentioned as potential suitor. Realistic return is a 3rd or 4th-round pick given the contract baggage. Eagles may opt to keep him for 2026 at the manageable $23.4M cap hit rather than absorb $43.5M in dead money.

players.name: DeVonta Smith
players.performance_evidence: Led the Eagles with 1,008 receiving yards in 2025 (77 receptions). Extended in April 2024 for 3yr/$75M with $51M guaranteed. Consistently productive — has been the team's most reliable receiver. Heisman Trophy winner. Cap hit of only $10.7M in 2026 is excellent value.

trade_value.reasoning: 27-year-old WR2 with Pro Bowl production on a team-friendly cap hit ($10.7M in 2026). Three years of control at $25M AAV. The catch is $35M dead cap makes a trade nearly impossible — the Eagles would lose cap space by trading him. Pure talent value is a late 1st, but dead cap locks him in place.

players.availability_reasoning: $35M dead cap exceeds his cap hit by $24M — trading him would ADD cap burden. Plus he's one of the offense's most important players and still in his prime. Absolutely no scenario where he's moved. If Brown leaves, Smith becomes even more critical.
players.injury_notes: Dealt with arm injury that caused him to miss 7 weeks in 2025. Has been relatively durable otherwise throughout career.

players.name: Johnny Wilson
players.performance_evidence: 6-foot-7 WR drafted in the 6th round in 2024. Carved out a WR3 role due to his red zone size. Limited stats but Eagles are developing him. Minimal production — mostly a big-bodied situational player.

trade_value.reasoning: Late-round pick with size but limited proven production. Worth a 7th-round pick or conditional late pick at most. The appeal is the physical profile on a cheap deal.

players.availability_reasoning: Depth WR on a minimum deal. Not a priority to keep. Would be traded for any mid-to-late round pick if the Eagles needed to clear a roster spot.

players.name: Grant Calcaterra
players.performance_evidence: Stepped into starting TE role after Dallas Goedert's expected departure. Re-signed on a one-year deal. Limited receiving production — more of a blocking/receiving hybrid. Not a dynamic pass catcher but adequate starter.

trade_value.reasoning: Below-average starting TE on a one-year deal. Minimal trade value — a team would need to be desperate at TE to offer anything more than a conditional late pick.

players.availability_reasoning: One-year deal, replacement-level starter. Eagles just signed him and Johnny Mundt to fill the void left by Goedert. Would trade for any pick if they find an upgrade.
players.injury_notes: History of concussions in college that caused him to briefly retire from football. Has been healthy in the NFL.

players.name: Johnny Mundt
players.performance_evidence: Veteran blocking TE signed from Jacksonville in 2026 free agency. Career journeyman with stints in LA, Minnesota, Cleveland, Jacksonville. Primarily a blocker. Minimal receiving production throughout career.

trade_value.reasoning: 30-year-old veteran blocking TE on a minimum deal. Essentially no trade value.

players.availability_reasoning: Journeyman on a minimum deal. Would be released before traded. No trade market.

players.name: Jordan Mailata
players.performance_evidence: Dominant left tackle who has anchored the Eagles' elite O-line. Signed through 2028. Australian rugby convert who became one of the NFL's best LTs. Pro Bowl caliber when healthy. Key part of the best O-line in football over the past 3 seasons.

trade_value.reasoning: Pro Bowl LT in his prime on a fair deal with 3 years of control. Elite positional value at OT. Dead cap ($22M) exceeds cap hit and makes a trade cap-negative. Worth a late 1st in a vacuum but financially unmovable.

players.availability_reasoning: Cornerstone of the O-line, signed long-term. $22M dead cap makes trade cap-negative. The Eagles would be insane to move him, especially with Lane Johnson aging.
players.injury_notes: Has dealt with various injuries but has been a reliable starter when healthy. Played 15+ games in each of the last 3 seasons.

players.name: Landon Dickerson
players.performance_evidence: Three consecutive Pro Bowl selections before 2025. Injuries limited his 2025 — not a Pro Bowler last season. Considered retirement this offseason before announcing his return. Just agreed to a revised 2-year deal worth ~$36M. Tough, dependable when healthy. Tied franchise record for Pro Bowl selections at guard.

trade_value.reasoning: Three-time Pro Bowl guard but significant injury concerns and briefly considered retirement. Revised contract reduces financial risk. Worth an early 3rd based on talent, but health questions cap the value. Dead cap ($15M) also limits trade feasibility.

players.availability_reasoning: Just re-committed to the team after considering retirement, and agreed to a revised deal. Eagles want him as part of their O-line core. Would need a significant offer to consider moving him, and the dead cap makes it unlikely. Not being shopped, but the injury concerns mean he has a price.
players.injury_notes: Extensive injury history: two torn ACLs in college, ankle issues, knee and back problems in 2025. Considered retirement. The injuries are the biggest question mark on this roster.

players.name: Cam Jurgens
players.performance_evidence: Pro Bowl selection in 2024 and 2025. Extended through 2029 in April 2025. Youngest Eagles center to earn Pro Bowl honors since 1953. Athletic center who excels in the run game and helped fuel Barkley's record-setting season. $8.5M cap hit in 2026 is a steal.

trade_value.reasoning: 25-year-old Pro Bowl center on a team-friendly cap hit with 4 years of control. One of the best young centers in football. Dead cap ($30M) makes a trade financially ruinous. Theoretical value is a mid-to-late 1st.

players.availability_reasoning: Just extended. 25 years old. Pro Bowl center. $30M dead cap. Four years of control at below-market rate. This is the definition of untouchable.
players.injury_notes: Back procedure after Super Bowl LIX. Back issues are a concern for a center long-term but played through them and earned Pro Bowl honors.

players.name: Tyler Steen
players.performance_evidence: Starting RG entering his 4th year. Adequate starter — not a star but competent within a strong O-line unit. Benefits from playing alongside elite OL talent. Third-round pick who has held the job.

trade_value.reasoning: Cheap starting guard with 2 years of control on a rookie deal. Average starter but excellent value at $1.5M cap hit. Worth a late 4th/early 5th to a guard-needy team.

players.availability_reasoning: Starting guard on a cheap deal. Eagles wouldn't actively shop him but would consider offers if they had a replacement ready. Not a priority to move but not untouchable either.

players.name: Lane Johnson
players.performance_evidence: Five-time All-Pro, two-time Super Bowl champion. But missed the last 7 games of 2025 and the Wild Card game with ankle injury. Approaching 36. Spotrac flagged him as heading toward the backside of his career. Still capable when healthy but durability is a growing concern. Three years, $60M remaining with zero guarantees.

trade_value.reasoning: Still a quality RT when healthy but age (36) and missed time are red flags. $40.2M dead cap vs $24M cap hit means trading him would cost the Eagles $16M MORE in cap space. Effectively untradeable due to dead cap math, not talent.

players.availability_reasoning: Not untouchable because of talent — untouchable because of dead cap. $40.2M dead cap on a $24M cap hit means the Eagles would lose $16M in cap by trading him. The team needs him to return healthy for 2026. Post-June 1, they could save ~$9M, but beat reporters note replacement at RT is a top priority in the draft.
players.injury_notes: Missed last 7 regular season games and Wild Card with ankle injury. At 36, recovery and durability are major concerns. Has battled various injuries throughout his career but was consistently elite when on the field.
players.trade_buzz: Beat reporters have flagged finding his replacement as a top draft priority. He's coming back for 2026 but the Eagles need to plan for the post-Johnson era immediately.

players.name: Moro Ojomo
players.performance_evidence: Breakout 2025: 6 sacks, 12 QB hits, 6 TFLs, 38 tackles, 54 pressures (2nd on team), 740 snaps in 17 games (9 starts). PFF overall grade ranked 26th among 127 IDLs. Higher PFF pass-rush grade than both Carter and Davis. 7th-round pick who has become a legit starter. Changed agents to Athletes First ahead of contract year — signaling big-money expectations.

trade_value.reasoning: 25-year-old emerging DT on a final rookie-deal year at under $4M. Outstanding value but only 1 year of control. Worth an early 3rd based on production and age, but the expiring contract caps value — acquiring team gets one year before he hits free agency demanding $20M+/year.

players.availability_reasoning: Eagles have Davis (extended) and Carter (extension imminent) ahead of him. Ojomo is in his contract year and keeping all three DTs long-term is nearly impossible per multiple beat reporters. He's the most likely of the three to be traded, but the Eagles would want significant compensation for a player who was their 2nd-best pass rusher.
players.injury_notes: Concussion during 2025 season but missed no games. Generally durable.
players.trade_buzz: Changed agents to Athletes First, signaling he's preparing for a big payday. Beat reporters have noted the Eagles can't keep all three DTs long-term. Ojomo may be the odd man out after Carter and Davis extensions.

players.name: Jordan Davis
players.performance_evidence: Just signed a 3-year, $78M extension with $65M guaranteed — highest-paid nose tackle in NFL history. Breakout 2025 season: career-high 72 tackles (3rd among DTs), 4.5 sacks, led all IDLs in run stops (34). Played career-high 61% of defensive snaps. PFF run defense grade of 71.6 (11th among IDL). Still a two-down player (only 87 3rd-down snaps) being paid like a three-down star.

trade_value.reasoning: Just extended for $78M. 27 years old with 4 years of control. Elite nose tackle. Dead cap ($60M) obliterates any trade possibility. Theoretical value is a late 1st but he literally cannot be traded.

players.availability_reasoning: Signed a $78M extension 5 days ago with $65M guaranteed. $60M dead cap. This player was just locked in as a defensive cornerstone through 2029. Absolutely untouchable.
players.injury_notes: Conditioning was a concern early in career but got in elite shape in 2024-2025. No significant injuries.

players.name: Jalen Carter
players.performance_evidence: 2024 Pro Bowl and All-Pro: 42 tackles, 4.5 sacks, 16 QB hits, 6 PBUs. 2025 was a regression — ejected in opener for spitting on Dak Prescott, shoulder injuries caused him to miss 4 games. Still supremely talented but inconsistent. Eagles and Carter are reportedly in active extension talks for a deal that could be $35M+/year. Fifth-year option ($27.1M) available.

trade_value.reasoning: 24-year-old former All-Pro DT on a cheap $6.9M cap hit with 2 years of team control (including 5th-year option). Even with the 2025 regression, the talent is undeniable. Worth a top-10 pick equivalent. If the Eagles traded him — which they almost certainly won't — they'd demand multiple 1sts.

players.availability_reasoning: Eagles have received trade calls per multiple reports, but insiders say this is likely a negotiating tactic during extension talks. The team views Carter as foundational and wants to extend him — it's 'a matter of when, not if' per beat reporters. However, the 2025 regression, spitting incident, and maturity concerns give the Eagles slight leverage. If talks break down, a trade becomes conceivable but unlikely. Price would be astronomical.
players.injury_notes: Shoulder injuries in 2025 required procedures and caused him to miss 4 games. Never looked fully healthy.
players.character_notes: Ejected in 2025 opener for spitting on Dak Prescott. Maturity and conditioning concerns have been reported. Draft stock dropped in 2023 due to involvement in fatal accident at Georgia. Off-field issues are a real factor in negotiations but Eagles believe he's maturing.
players.trade_buzz: Eagles reportedly received trade calls but are in active extension discussions per 97.5 The Fanatic. Agent Drew Rosenhaus reportedly seeking $35M/year; Eagles offering $29-30M. Beat reporters expect a deal to get done. Trade rumors appear to be a negotiating tactic rather than genuine availability.

players.name: Nolan Smith Jr.
players.performance_evidence: 2024 breakout: 6.5 sacks, 11 QB hits, 24 pressures. But 2025 was derailed by triceps injury — missed 5 games on IR, finished with only 3 sacks and 31 tackles in 12 games. PFF grade of 67.7 (average). 4.5 career playoff sacks. Spotrac noted it may be difficult to justify exercising his ~$19M fifth-year option given the injury concerns. Athletic freak (4.39 40, 41.5" vertical at 238 lbs) but hasn't put it together consistently.

trade_value.reasoning: 25-year-old EDGE on a cheap deal with elite physical traits and 2 years of control. The 2024 postseason showed his upside but the triceps injury and inconsistent regular season cap his value. Worth a late 2nd/early 3rd to a team betting on the physical tools.

players.availability_reasoning: Eagles have a decision to make on his 5th-year option (~$19M) by May 1. Given the injury history and inconsistency, they may decline it, which could make him available. However, EDGE is a premium position and they just lost Jaelan Phillips in free agency. He's needed for 2026 but could be moved if the Eagles draft an EDGE early.
players.injury_notes: Torn triceps requiring surgery after Super Bowl LIX (Feb 2025). Aggravated the surgically repaired triceps in Week 3 of 2025, missed 5 games on IR. Significant concern for a player whose game relies on explosiveness.

players.name: Zack Baun
players.performance_evidence: First-Team All-Pro 2024: 150+ tackles, 3.5 sacks, 5 forced fumbles, INT, DPOY finalist. Intercepted Mahomes in Super Bowl LIX. In 2025: continued as team's defensive leader though production dipped slightly from historic 2024. Signed 3yr/$51M extension with $34M guaranteed. Transformed from special teamer in New Orleans to one of the best LBs in football under Vic Fangio.

trade_value.reasoning: All-Pro LB on a $17M AAV deal with 2 years of control. Elite production at a position that typically doesn't command premium trade value. At 29, he's in his prime but the clock is ticking. Dead cap ($28M) makes a trade financially devastating. Worth a late 1st but immovable.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed a $51M extension specifically to keep him. $28M dead cap. All-Pro who is the defensive leader and captain. The Eagles built their defense around him. Zero chance of being traded.

players.name: Jihaad Campbell
players.performance_evidence: 2025 PFWA All-Rookie Team. 76 tackles, 11 QB pressures, 1 INT, 1 FF, 1 FR in 17 games (10 starts). Named to the starting lineup alongside Baun. Showed he could play all three phases — run defense, coverage (59.4 passer rating allowed vs Bills), and pass rush. Local kid from New Jersey. Still developing but the foundation is exceptional for a rookie.

trade_value.reasoning: 22-year-old first-round LB on a cheap rookie deal with 4 years of control. All-Rookie team production with clear upside. Dead cap ($8M) makes a trade unlikely. Worth a mid-2nd but the Eagles view him as a long-term starter next to Baun.

players.availability_reasoning: First-round pick entering year 2 with 4 years of control at $3.5M/year. Core piece of the defense going forward, especially after Nakobe Dean left in free agency. The Eagles drafted him to be Baun's long-term partner. Not being moved under any circumstances.

players.name: Jalyx Hunt
players.performance_evidence: Led the Eagles with 6.5 sacks AND 3 INTs in 2025 — first player in franchise history to lead team in both categories in a season. Also had 52 tackles, 2 forced fumbles, 1 fumble recovery. PFF grade of 68.1. Barely played as a rookie before exploding in year 2 after Sweat/Huff departures. Cornell safety-turned-EDGE with rare athleticism (34.25" arms, 128" broad jump).

trade_value.reasoning: 25-year-old ascending EDGE rusher on a dirt-cheap 3rd-round rookie deal with 3 years of control. Led the team in sacks. The production and contract are an incredible combination. Worth a mid-2nd to any team needing pass rush, but Eagles desperately need him after losing Phillips.

players.availability_reasoning: The Eagles just lost Jaelan Phillips to Carolina ($120M deal). They also lost Josh Sweat and Za'Darius Smith. Hunt is now their most productive and cost-controlled EDGE rusher. Three years of control at $1.4M/year. Trading him would be organizational malpractice given the pass-rush void. Absolutely untouchable.

players.name: Riq Woolen
players.performance_evidence: Just signed 1-year, up to $15M deal ($12M base + $3M incentives). Pro Bowl as a rookie in 2022 (6 INTs). Inconsistent since — was benched at times in Seattle in both 2023 and 2024/2025. 27.6% man coverage completion rate (lowest since 2018 per Next Gen Stats) over final 12 weeks of 2025. Super Bowl champion with Seattle. 6-4, 210 with 4.26 speed. Elite physical tools but reliability is a question mark.

trade_value.reasoning: One-year prove-it deal limits trade value. Eagles just signed him to address a major need at CB2. He has elite physical tools but inconsistency is why he only got a 1-year deal. Worth a 4th-round pick at most given the contract structure, but the Eagles need him too much to trade him.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed to fill a specific need (CB2 opposite Mitchell). Prove-it deal means the Eagles are invested but not locked in long-term. Would need a strong offer (3rd-round pick) to consider moving him, and only if they had a replacement ready. More likely he plays out the year and tests free agency again.
players.injury_notes: No significant injuries but was benched for performance/team rule violations in Seattle, not injury.
players.character_notes: Benched by two different coaching staffs in Seattle — once by Pete Carroll for poor tackling, once by Mike Macdonald for undisclosed team rule violations. The one-year deal reflects teams' caution about his reliability and consistency.

players.name: Quinyon Mitchell
players.performance_evidence: First-Team All-Pro in both 2024 and 2025 — his first two NFL seasons. Considered one of the best outside CBs in football. Travels with opponent's top WR and frequently played in zero coverage with no safety help. First Eagles CB named First-Team All-Pro since Lito Sheppard 20+ years ago. Eligible for extension after 2026 season — expected to reset the CB market north of $31M/year.

trade_value.reasoning: 24-year-old two-time First-Team All-Pro CB on a rookie deal with 3 years of control at $3.2M/year. This is one of the most valuable contracts in the NFL. Worth multiple 1sts in any trade scenario. Think Jalen Ramsey-level return or better. But the Eagles would never do it.

players.availability_reasoning: Two-time All-Pro in his first two seasons. 24 years old. Three years of elite production at $3.2M/year. This is the single most valuable non-QB asset on the Eagles roster and arguably one of the most valuable in the NFL. Untouchable in every conceivable scenario.

players.name: Cooper DeJean
players.performance_evidence: First-Team All-Pro in 2025 (second season). Versatile hybrid CB who plays slot, outside, and has been used at safety. Played 57% of 2025 snaps in the slot, down from 70% as a rookie. Physical tackler, excellent in run support. Alongside Mitchell, forms arguably the best CB duo in football. Eligible for extension after 2026 — could reset the slot/hybrid CB market.

trade_value.reasoning: 22-year-old First-Team All-Pro on a 2nd-round rookie deal at $2.2M/year with 3 years of control. Almost as valuable as Mitchell. The versatility (slot, outside, safety) makes him scheme-proof. Worth multiple 1sts but utterly untradeable.

players.availability_reasoning: All-Pro at 22 years old on a dirt-cheap deal. Three years of control. The Eagles' entire defensive secondary is built around Mitchell and DeJean. Not available at any price.

players.name: Michael Carter II
players.performance_evidence: Acquired from Jets at 2025 trade deadline. Played only 106 defensive snaps after acquisition. Former good slot CB in New York who the Eagles are trying to convert to safety. Was on a 3yr/$30M deal but only $1.38M is guaranteed — Eagles can cut him to save $8.7M. Barely saw the field in Philly.

trade_value.reasoning: Massively overpaid relative to his Eagles role. $10.1M cap hit for a player who played 106 snaps. The contract is the problem — no team would trade for this cap number when the Eagles are likely to cut him. Worth a conditional late pick at most, but more likely to be released.

players.availability_reasoning: Spotrac, OTC, and every beat reporter has flagged Carter as the most obvious cut candidate. Only $1.38M dead cap vs $8.7M in savings. NBC Sports Philly called cutting him 'an obvious move.' The Eagles may try to get anything in trade but will almost certainly release him if they can't.
players.trade_buzz: Multiple outlets expect the Eagles to release Carter before the start of the league year. Spotrac identified him as a cap casualty candidate. If he's not released, he'd need to take a massive pay cut to stay.

players.name: Andrew Mukuba
players.performance_evidence: Started 10 games as a rookie in 2025: 46 tackles, 2 INTs (including one off Mahomes in Week 2), 0.5 sacks. Played 80%+ defensive snaps in 10 of 11 games. Season ended with fractured ankle requiring surgery in November. CBS and beat reporters expect him to reclaim starting role when healthy. Born in Zimbabwe, immigrated to US at age 9.

trade_value.reasoning: 23-year-old starting safety on a cheap 2nd-round rookie deal with 4 years of control. Showed real promise as a rookie before the ankle fracture. Dead cap ($5M) and team need (lost Blankenship in FA) make him essential. Worth an early 3rd but the Eagles can't afford to lose him.

players.availability_reasoning: The Eagles just lost Reed Blankenship to Houston in free agency. Mukuba is now the only established starting safety on the roster. 23 years old with 4 years of cheap control. The Eagles desperately need him healthy for 2026. Not available at any price.
players.injury_notes: Fractured right ankle in November 2025, underwent surgery. Expected to be ready for start of 2026 season per CBS Sports. The fracture is a concern but he's young and should recover fully.
